meta.title: HPB-TCT Trading Bot — Complete Operational Workflow Logic Map
meta.description: Machine-readable hierarchical map of all decision logic in the Schematics-5B trading engine. Covers market bias determination, range validation, liquidity checks, session scaling, counter-bias protection, execution confidence, and trade management.

changelog.1.1: Added MSCE (Multi-Session Confidence Engine) session multipliers for Asia, London, and NY sessions. Expanded step 7.8 with full per-session definitions and rationale. Added step 8.4 to apply MSCE multiplier to execution confidence before threshold gating.
changelog.1.2: Implemented full Range Integrity Gate (RIG) at step 6.7. Replaced 3-condition stub with 4-sub-step implementation: 6.7.1 HTF range integrity assessment (duration, DL2 violations, displacement), 6.7.2 counter-bias detection, 6.7.3 CHoCH domino bypass check (L3→L2→L1 + model-flow), 6.7.4 session-adjusted RIG verdict. Added RIG constants block with per-session displacement thresholds. Updated step 6.0 inputs.
changelog.1.3: Aggregated execution confidence correctly. Added COMPOSITE_SCORE_RAW_MAX(110), COMPOSITE_SCORE_MAX(100), and RIG_CONFIDENCE_PENALTIES constants. Fixed step 7.0 with explicit score formula, component max table, and score cap annotation. Removed threshold pre-check from step 7.0 conditions. Expanded step 8.4 into EXECUTION_CONFIDENCE_AGGREGATION: unified three-factor formula (min(score,100) × session_multiplier × rig_confidence_factor), single authoritative threshold gate, full confidence_components schema, worked examples for all session×RIG combinations. Removed redundant threshold pre-filter from step 8.0.

constants._annotation_msce: MSCE = Multi-Session Confidence Engine. Multipliers are applied to the composite_score AFTER all tree scoring to produce an adjusted_confidence value. This adjusted value is what is compared against the entry threshold in step 8.4. The 1% fixed risk rule (step 8.1) is never touched by MSCE — only entry gating changes.

MSCE_SESSION_MULTIPLIERS._note: UTC windows are non-overlapping. Priority order for overlap resolution: london_ny > ny > london > asia > off_hours.

asia.label: Asia session
asia.rationale: Low liquidity, thin order books, frequent stop-hunts with no follow-through. Setups have higher false-positive rate. Confidence reduced.

london.label: London session
london.rationale: European institutional participation begins. Volume ramps, FVGs fill reliably, BOS signals carry more weight. Confidence elevated.

london_ny.label: London/NY overlap
london_ny.rationale: Peak global liquidity window. Both European and US institutions active simultaneously. Highest probability of clean directional moves with follow-through. Maximum confidence.

ny.label: NY session
ny.rationale: US institutions dominate. High volume, clear price discovery. Strong momentum continuation. Confidence elevated but below overlap peak.

off_hours.label: NY close / off-hours
off_hours.rationale: NY session winding down. Position squaring creates noise. Setups taken here often reverse at Asia open. Confidence slightly reduced.

constants._annotation_msce_threshold: Thresholds remain the same numeric values but are compared against adjusted_confidence = composite_score × session_multiplier, not raw composite_score. This means a 60-pt raw score in Asia (×0.85) produces adjusted_confidence=51 and FAILS the v2 threshold, while the same score in London/NY overlap (×1.25) produces 75 and passes comfortably.
constants._annotation_rig: RIG = Range Integrity Gate. Protects against counter-bias trades entered while an HTF range is still structurally intact. A counter-bias trade is one whose direction is OPPOSITE to htf_bias while the HTF range has not been broken. The gate is bypassed when CHoCH is confirmed across 3 timeframe levels (L3→L2→L1) or when a model-flow conversion signals genuine intent change.
constants._annotation_rig_duration: A range must have been holding for at least 24 hours to be considered 'intact' for RIG purposes. Younger ranges have not had time to build structural significance.
constants._annotation_rig_displacement: Default: if current price has moved ≥ 25% of range size AWAY from the nearest boundary, the range is likely breaking and counter-bias entries become permitted. Session-specific overrides apply (see RIG_DISPLACEMENT_SESSION_THRESHOLDS).

RIG_DISPLACEMENT_SESSION_THRESHOLDS._note: Per-session displacement thresholds for the RIG verdict (step 6.7.4). Lower value = stricter gate = harder to get a counter-bias trade approved. Asia is strictest because counter-bias moves rarely follow through in low-liquidity sessions.

constants._annotation_rig_choch: Full CHoCH bypass requires BOS confirmation on all 3 levels: L3 (1D), L2 (4H), L1 (1H) in the direction of the local trade. 2-of-3 grants a partial bypass (displacement threshold halved). Fewer than 2 = no bypass.
constants._annotation_rig_dl2: When checking whether a DL2 close violation was 'accepted back', look at the next N candles (default 3). If price has not returned inside DL2 within 3 candles → TRUE BREAK → range is no longer intact.
constants._annotation_rig_proximity: Price is considered 'at the range boundary' (for displacement calc) if it is within 0.5% of range_high or range_low. This prevents false high-displacement readings when price is just at the edge.
constants._annotation_exec_confidence: Execution confidence is the single authoritative value compared against the entry threshold at step 8.4. It is derived by applying sequential multipliers to the capped composite_score: execution_confidence = min(composite_score, COMPOSITE_SCORE_MAX) × session_multiplier × rig_confidence_factor. This aggregates session context (MSCE) and counter-bias risk (RIG) into one number before the threshold gate.
constants._annotation_raw_max: Theoretical maximum raw score before capping: T1(20)+T2(20)+T3(15)+T4(20)+T5(15)+T6(5)+RR_bonus(5)+session_additive(10) = 110. The cap at 100 prevents the session additive from inflating confidence above the scoring ceiling.
constants._annotation_score_max: Raw composite_score is capped at 100 before any multipliers are applied. This ensures the session_multiplier and rig_confidence_factor always operate on a bounded [0, 100] input.

RIG_CONFIDENCE_PENALTIES._note: Multiplied into execution_confidence at step 8.4 based on the rig_verdict_path from step 6.7.4. Lower factor = more penalty = less confidence in the trade. Aligned trades carry no penalty. Counter-bias trades carry graduated penalties based on the strength of structural justification.

aligned_trade.rationale: With-bias trade — no counter-bias risk, no penalty

range_boundary_broken.rationale: HTF range already violated — structural protection lifted, trade evaluated on its own merit

choch_bypass:model_flow.rationale: Model conversion (M1→M2 or schematic flip) — strong structural signal but still counter-bias; small 3% confidence reduction

choch_bypass:full_domino.rationale: L3+L2+L1 CHoCH all confirmed — high conviction but counter-bias; 5% confidence reduction

choch_bypass:partial_domino.rationale: Only 2-of-3 CHoCH levels confirmed — moderate conviction; 10% confidence reduction

displacement_sufficient.rationale: Counter-bias trade that passed via price displacement alone — no CHoCH structural support; 15% confidence reduction (highest penalty)

constants._annotation_exec_confidence_formula: Full execution confidence chain: (1) raw_score = sum of T1-T6 + rr_bonus + session_additive; (2) capped_score = min(raw_score, 100); (3) session_adjusted = capped_score × session_multiplier; (4) execution_confidence = session_adjusted × rig_confidence_factor. Gate: execution_confidence >= ENTRY_THRESHOLD (60 v2, 50 v1) → ENTER. Single check at step 8.4 — no earlier threshold pre-filter.

workflow.name: AUTO_SCAN_LOOP
workflow.decision_logic: Polling loop that fires every 60 seconds. Acquires async lock to prevent concurrent scans. Checks if a position is already open — if yes, routes to trade management (step 8.0). If no position, proceeds to scan for new setups.
outputs: triggers full pipeline per symbol per MTF timeframe

conditions.gate_position_open: If current_trade is not None → skip to step 8.0 (manage existing position)
conditions.gate_duplicate_cooldown: If same symbol+price (±0.2%) was evaluated within 300s → skip

children.name: HTF_BIAS_DETERMINATION
children.decision_logic: Determines the Higher Time Frame directional bias using 6-candle-rule pivot detection on daily candles. Identifies swing highs and swing lows, then classifies the last two of each to determine market structure direction. Structure break confirmation requires a candle CLOSE beyond the prior swing level.

conditions.cache_hit: If cached bias exists and TTL not expired (24h for directional, 1h for neutral) → return cached value without recomputation
conditions.bullish_classification: Last 2 swing highs form Higher High AND last 2 swing lows form Higher Low → 'bullish'
conditions.bearish_classification: Last 2 swing highs form Lower High AND last 2 swing lows form Lower Low → 'bearish'
conditions.structure_break_confirmation: Close beyond prior swing pivot confirms direction; wick-only does not confirm

children.name: SIX_CANDLE_RULE_PIVOT_DETECTION
children.decision_logic: Scans candles for pivot highs and lows using the 6-candle rule: a pivot HIGH requires 2+ consecutive bullish candles followed by 2+ consecutive bearish candles. A pivot LOW requires the inverse. Inside bars (close == open or body < threshold) do NOT count toward the sequence but do NOT break it either.

conditions.min_shoulder_candles: At least 2 consecutive candles in each direction (3-candle shoulder each side)
conditions.inside_bar_handling: Inside bars are skipped — they neither count nor reset the consecutive sequence

children.name: STRUCTURE_CLASSIFICATION
children.decision_logic: Compares the last two swing highs and last two swing lows to classify as HH (Higher High), HL (Higher Low), LH (Lower High), or LL (Lower Low). These four labels determine the bias.

children.name: RANGE_FORMATION_AND_VALIDATION
children.decision_logic: Identifies horizontal consolidation ranges by finding swing high/low boundaries. Calculates Equilibrium (EQ) and Deviation Limits (DL2). Validates that the range is genuinely horizontal (not a V-shaped impulse) and that EQ has been touched.

conditions.EQ_touch_required: At least one candle must close within 5% of EQ — confirms range is being respected
conditions.horizontal_validation: At least 60% of closes must stay within range boundaries AND direction changes ≥ 15% of candle count AND EQ crossings ≥ 10% of candle count
conditions.tap1_identification: Tap1 = first definitive touch of range low (accumulation) or range high (distribution)

children.name: RANGE_QUALITY_SCORING
children.decision_logic: Scores the quality of the identified range by checking horizontal character, liquidity stacking at boundaries, and EQ interaction frequency.

conditions.liquidity_stacking: 2+ equal highs (within 0.1%) at range_high OR 2+ equal lows at range_low → indicates stop-loss clustering
conditions.V_shape_rejection: If most price action is directional with < 15% direction changes → NOT a valid range

children.name: DEVIATION_AND_SCHEMATIC_DETECTION
children.decision_logic: After identifying Tap1 (first touch of range boundary), searches for Tap2 (first deviation beyond range) and Tap3 (deeper deviation or higher-low retest). Classifies as Model 1 or Model 2 based on the relationship between Tap2 and Tap3. Validates that deviations stay within DL2.

conditions.tap2_requirement: Tap2 = first deviation beyond range boundary that comes BACK inside the range. Must NOT close beyond DL2.
conditions.model_1_classification: Tap3 goes LOWER than Tap2 (accumulation) or HIGHER than Tap2 (distribution) — creates a lower low / higher high outside range
conditions.model_2_classification: Tap3 is a HIGHER LOW relative to Tap2 (accumulation) that grabs extreme liquidity (first MS low after Tap2) OR mitigates extreme demand/supply zone
conditions.DL2_gate: If any candle CLOSES beyond DL2 AND does not get accepted back within 3 candles → TRUE BREAK → schematic INVALID
conditions.tap_spacing_requirement: Time gap between Tap2 and Tap3 must be ≥ 30% of the gap between Tap1 and Tap2
conditions.v2_tolerance: In v2 scoring, allow close slightly beyond DL2 if within 0.3% tolerance (softer gate)

children.name: MODEL_1_DETECTION
children.decision_logic: Searches for a Tap3 that creates a LOWER LOW below Tap2 (for accumulation) or HIGHER HIGH above Tap2 (for distribution). This is the classic spring/upthrust pattern.

conditions.accumulation_model_1: tap3_low < tap2_low AND tap3_close > range_low (accepted back)
conditions.distribution_model_1: tap3_high > tap2_high AND tap3_close < range_high (accepted back)

children.name: MODEL_2_DETECTION
children.decision_logic: Searches for a Tap3 that forms a HIGHER LOW relative to Tap2 (accumulation) — does NOT make new low. Must grab either extreme liquidity (first MS low after Tap2) or mitigate extreme demand zone (S/D).

conditions.liquidity_grab_or_sd: Must satisfy at least one: (a) price reaches extreme liquidity level, OR (b) price enters extreme S/D zone

children.name: BOS_CONFIRMATION_AND_LTF_CASCADE
children.decision_logic: After Tap3, searches for a Break of Structure (BOS) — a candle that CLOSES above the highest point between Tap2 and Tap3 (bullish) or below the lowest point (bearish). Cascades through lower timeframes (5m → 1m) to find the earliest confirmation for best risk-reward. Lowest TF BOS overwrites higher TF BOS.

conditions.mtf_bos_search: On the schematic timeframe, find first candle CLOSE beyond the swing high/low between Tap2-Tap3
conditions.ltf_cascade: After MTF BOS found, search 5m then 1m for earlier BOS — LOWEST TF wins (best R:R)
conditions.bos_staleness_check: BOS must occur within MAX_STALE candles of Tap3 (varies by TF: 1d=2, 4h=3, 1h=3, 30m=4, 5m=10, 1m=30). If exceeded → STALE → REJECT
conditions.ltf_internal_structure: Primary: search for BOS using internal structure swings between Tap2-Tap3 timestamps
conditions.ltf_fallback: If no internal structure, use post-Tap3 swings as fallback BOS points
conditions.eq_filter_v1: In v1 mode: BOS must be below EQ for accumulation, above EQ for distribution
conditions.hard_gate: If no BOS found on any timeframe → HARD FAIL → cannot enter trade

children.name: LTF_BOS_REFINEMENT
children.decision_logic: Fetches LTF candles around the MTF BOS zone. Identifies LTF swing structure and finds first LTF BOS (close beyond LTF swing). If found, uses LTF BOS price as entry and LTF swing extreme as stop — tighter risk, better R:R.

conditions.1m_override: If 1m BOS found, it overwrites 5m BOS (tighter = better)
conditions.no_ltf_found: If no LTF BOS → fall back to MTF BOS levels

children.name: GATING_CHECKS
inputs: schematic from step 4.0 (direction, model_type, tap prices)
htf_range_high, htf_range_low, htf_EQ, htf_DL2_above, htf_DL2_below, range_start_timestamp from step 3.0
HTF candles (1D, 4H, 1H) for RIG CHoCH check
active_session from step 7.8 (for RIG session-adjusted thresholds)
children.decision_logic: Series of hard gates that must ALL pass before scoring proceeds. Any single hard gate failure results in immediate rejection — the trade is NOT taken regardless of other factors. Gate 6.7 (RIG) is the final gate and uses session-aware displacement thresholds to block counter-bias trades in intact HTF ranges.

conditions.HARD_GATES: ALL must pass or trade is rejected
conditions.gate_order: 6.1 HTF alignment → 6.2 BOS confirmed → 6.3 R:R ≥ 1.5 → 6.4 S/D zone → 6.5 liquidity sweep → 6.6 TCT 5A validity → 6.7 RIG counter-bias protection

children.name: HTF_ALIGNMENT_GATE
children.decision_logic: Checks that the trade direction aligns with HTF bias. Counter-bias trades (e.g., bearish setup during bullish HTF) are typically rejected.

conditions.fail_hard: htf_bias is OPPOSITE to schematic.direction → REJECT
conditions.exception_neutral: htf_bias == 'neutral' → HARD FAIL in v2 (neutral = uncertain = no entry)
conditions.exception_trend_shift: If CHoCH (Change of Character) detected in HTF → counter-bias entry MAY be allowed

children.name: BOS_CONFIRMATION_GATE
children.decision_logic: Requires confirmed BOS. Without structural confirmation, no trade.

children.name: RISK_REWARD_GATE
children.decision_logic: Calculates live risk-to-reward ratio. Hard floor of 1.5. Trades below this threshold are never taken.

conditions.calculation: live_risk = abs(entry_price - stop_price); live_reward = abs(target_price - entry_price); rr = live_reward / live_risk

children.name: SUPPLY_DEMAND_VALIDATION_GATE
children.decision_logic: Verifies that a valid supply or demand zone exists near Tap3. FVG is a 3-candle pattern leaving an unmitigated gap. OB is a swing-structure point (last opposing candle before impulse). At least one must be present in the correct zone.

conditions.for_bullish: Demand zone (FVG or OB) must exist BELOW or AT Tap3 level
conditions.for_bearish: Supply zone (FVG or OB) must exist ABOVE or AT Tap3 level
conditions.hard_gate: If NO FVG and NO OB near Tap3 → HARD FAIL (unless schematic already confirmed via other path)
conditions.fvg_definition: 3 consecutive candles where candle[1].low > candle[0].high (bullish) or candle[1].high < candle[0].low (bearish)

children.name: LIQUIDITY_SWEEP_VALIDATION_GATE
children.decision_logic: Classifies the liquidity event as SWEEP (acceptable) or TRUE BREAK (rejection). A sweep means price went beyond range but came back. A true break means price closed beyond DL2 and stayed there.

conditions.swept: Any candle low/high beyond range boundary but closes back inside → VALID sweep
conditions.deep_break: Candle CLOSES beyond DL2 but penetration ≥ 1% of range AND accepted back within ≥ 3 candles → VALID (soft pass)
conditions.true_break: Candle CLOSES beyond DL2 AND NOT accepted back within 3 candles → HARD FAIL
conditions.v2_tolerance: In v2, allow close slightly beyond DL2 if within 0.3% tolerance before classifying as true break

children.name: TCT_5A_SCHEMATIC_VALIDITY_GATE
children.decision_logic: Validates that the overall TCT 5A schematic structure is intact — range found, taps identified, model type classified, deviation within limits.

conditions.pass: schematic has valid range + valid taps + valid model type + deviation within DL2
conditions.fail_hard: Any core schematic element missing or invalid → HARD FAIL

children.name: COUNTER_BIAS_RANGE_INTEGRITY_GATE
children._annotation: RIG v1.2 — Full implementation. Expanded from a 3-condition stub into a 4-sub-step gate. Evaluates whether the HTF range is genuinely intact, whether the local trade is counter-bias, whether CHoCH provides a structural bypass, and applies session-specific displacement thresholds for the final verdict. This is a HARD GATE — any rejection is non-negotiable and stops the pipeline immediately.
inputs: htf_range_high, htf_range_low, htf_EQ, htf_DL2_above, htf_DL2_below from step 3.0
children.decision_logic: Runs four sequential sub-checks. If the trade is NOT counter-bias, the gate passes immediately without evaluating further. If counter-bias, checks whether the HTF range is intact, whether CHoCH provides a structural bypass, and applies session-adjusted thresholds to determine whether displacement is sufficient to allow entry.

conditions.fast_pass: htf_bias == schematic.direction OR htf_bias == 'neutral' → RIG not applicable → PASS immediately (no sub-checks needed)
conditions.hard_fail_final: is_counter_bias AND range_intact AND current_displacement_pct < effective_displacement_threshold AND choch_bypass_eligible == False → REJECT

children.name: HTF_RANGE_INTEGRITY_ASSESSMENT
children._annotation: NEW in v1.2. Measures the three dimensions of range integrity: time held, boundary violation history, and current price displacement from boundary. All three feed the final RIG verdict.
children.decision_logic: Computes range_duration_hours from range_start_timestamp to now. Scans all candles since range start for DL2 close violations: a violation is a candle that CLOSES beyond DL2 and is NOT accepted back within RIG_DL2_VIOLATION_LOOKBACK_CANDLES (3) candles. Computes current_displacement_pct as abs(current_price - nearest_range_boundary) / range_size. Range is 'intact' only if boundary_violation_count == 0 AND range_duration_hours >= RIG_MIN_DURATION_HOURS (24).

conditions.range_too_young: range_duration_hours < 24 → range_intact = False (range has not had time to build structural significance)
conditions.boundary_violation_definition: A candle CLOSES beyond DL2 (above DL2_above or below DL2_below) AND price does NOT return inside DL2 within the next 3 candles → counts as 1 violation
conditions.boundary_accepted_back: If a candle closes beyond DL2 but returns inside within 3 candles → NOT a violation (same logic as step 6.5 liquidity gate)
conditions.nearest_boundary: nearest_boundary = 'low' if abs(current_price - range_low) < abs(current_price - range_high), else 'high'
conditions.proximity_edge_case: If current_price is within RIG_BOUNDARY_PROXIMITY_PCT (0.5%) of range boundary → displacement treated as 0.0 (price is at boundary, not displaced)

children.name: COUNTER_BIAS_DETECTION
children._annotation: NEW in v1.2. Formally classifies whether the current trade setup conflicts with the HTF bias inside an intact range. This is the predicate that determines whether the remaining RIG sub-steps need to run.
children.decision_logic: A trade is counter-bias if its direction is opposite to HTF bias AND the HTF range is still intact. If the range is NOT intact (boundary broken), counter-bias detection is irrelevant — the range has already signaled a potential trend shift, so the trade is not protected.

conditions.bullish_range_bearish_trade: htf_bias == 'bullish' AND schematic.direction == 'bearish' AND range_intact == True → is_counter_bias = True, bias_conflict_type = 'bullish_range_bearish_trade'
conditions.bearish_range_bullish_trade: htf_bias == 'bearish' AND schematic.direction == 'bullish' AND range_intact == True → is_counter_bias = True, bias_conflict_type = 'bearish_range_bullish_trade'
conditions.aligned: htf_bias == schematic.direction → is_counter_bias = False (with-bias trade, RIG does not apply)
conditions.neutral_bias: htf_bias == 'neutral' → is_counter_bias = False (neutral bias has no protected direction; v2 hard-fails neutral at step 6.1 before reaching RIG)
conditions.broken_range_exception: range_intact == False → is_counter_bias = False (range boundary has been violated, structural protection is lifted)

children.name: CHOCH_DOMINO_BYPASS_CHECK
children._annotation: NEW in v1.2. Evaluates whether a multi-timeframe Change of Character (CHoCH) domino has confirmed, which would indicate the HTF range IS in fact breaking down despite appearing intact on surface metrics. L3→L2→L1 is the required confirmation cascade. Also handles model-flow bypass.
inputs: schematic.direction (to determine which direction BOS must be)
children.decision_logic: For each timeframe level, checks whether the most recent swing structure has produced a BOS (close beyond prior swing) in the direction of the local schematic. L3 = 1D, L2 = 4H, L1 = 1H. If all 3 levels confirm → full bypass. If 2 levels confirm → partial bypass (displacement threshold halved). Fewer than 2 = no bypass. Additionally checks for model-flow conversions (Model 1→2, acc→dist or dist→acc) which independently grant full bypass.

conditions.L3_choch: 1D timeframe: most recent swing structure shows a CLOSE beyond the prior daily swing in schematic.direction → L3 confirmed
conditions.L2_choch: 4H timeframe: most recent swing structure shows a CLOSE beyond the prior 4H swing in schematic.direction → L2 confirmed
conditions.L1_choch: 1H timeframe: most recent swing structure shows a CLOSE beyond the prior 1H swing in schematic.direction → L1 confirmed
conditions.full_domino_bypass: choch_level_count >= 3 → choch_bypass_eligible = True, choch_bypass_type = 'full_domino' → RIG BYPASSED
conditions.partial_domino_bypass: choch_level_count == 2 → choch_bypass_eligible = True, choch_bypass_type = 'partial_domino' → displacement threshold halved in step 6.7.4
conditions.model_flow_bypass_m1_to_m2: schematic_model_type converted from Model_1 → Model_2 since last evaluation → choch_bypass_eligible = True, choch_bypass_type = 'model_flow' (Model 2 implies range is absorbing counter-bias pressure)
conditions.model_flow_bypass_schematic_flip: schematic previously acc, now detected as dist (or vice versa) → choch_bypass_eligible = True, choch_bypass_type = 'model_flow' (schematic flip = clear market intent reversal)
conditions.bos_direction_requirement: For bullish schematic: BOS = close above prior swing HIGH. For bearish schematic: BOS = close below prior swing LOW. Wick-only does NOT count.

children.name: SESSION_ADJUSTED_RIG_VERDICT
children._annotation: NEW in v1.2. Applies session-specific displacement thresholds and renders the final hard-gate decision. This is the only step that can produce a RIG_HARD_FAIL rejection. Session-aware thresholds reflect that counter-bias moves are more dangerous in low-liquidity sessions (Asia) and more forgivable near session transitions where structure shifts are real.
children.decision_logic: Works through a priority-ordered decision tree. Fast passes exit immediately without computing thresholds. Only when the trade is confirmed counter-bias, the range is intact, and no CHoCH bypass is available does the gate compare current_displacement_pct against the session-adjusted threshold to reach a final verdict.
outputs: rig_verdict_path: string (which condition triggered the verdict)

conditions._evaluation_order: Conditions are checked in order. First match wins.
conditions.pass_3_full_choch_bypass: choch_bypass_eligible == True AND choch_bypass_type IN ['full_domino', 'model_flow'] → PASS. rig_verdict_path = 'choch_bypass:{choch_bypass_type}'
conditions.session_threshold_lookup: effective_displacement_threshold = RIG_DISPLACEMENT_SESSION_THRESHOLDS[active_session]: asia=0.15, london=0.25, london_ny=0.30, ny=0.28, off_hours=0.20
conditions.partial_choch_threshold_reduction: If choch_bypass_type == 'partial_domino' → effective_displacement_threshold = effective_displacement_threshold × 0.50 (halved — 2-of-3 levels confirmed, make it easier to pass)
conditions.fail_hard: current_displacement_pct < effective_displacement_threshold → HARD FAIL. rig_rejection_reason = 'RIG_COUNTER_BIAS_INTACT_RANGE: {bias_conflict_type}, session={active_session}, displacement={current_displacement_pct:.3f} < threshold={effective_displacement_threshold:.3f}, range_age={range_duration_hours:.1f}h'

session_threshold_rationale.asia_0.15: Asia session: counter-bias setups almost never follow through in thin markets. Require only 15% displacement — if price is this close to the boundary it is almost certainly just noise.
session_threshold_rationale.london_0.25: London standard: 25% displacement is the baseline before a counter-bias trade is considered structurally justified.
session_threshold_rationale.london_ny_0.30: London/NY overlap: highest-volume window, structure shifts ARE real here. Allow 30% displacement — a larger displacement is meaningful when liquidity is deep.
session_threshold_rationale.ny_0.28: NY session: similar to overlap but slightly tighter — late-session moves can reverse at close.
session_threshold_rationale.off_hours_0.20: Off-hours: position squaring creates noise. Tighter than standard to reduce false signals from thin-market moves.

children.name: COMPOSITE_SCORING
children._annotation: v1.3 — composite_score is now explicitly defined as the sum of all tree scores capped at COMPOSITE_SCORE_MAX (100). The session_additive IS included in this sum. The session_multiplier and rig_confidence_factor are NOT applied here — they are applied at step 8.4. This step outputs only the raw capped score and the per-tree breakdown for diagnostics.
children.decision_logic: Sums scores from all 8 sub-steps (T1–T6 + rr_bonus + session_additive) into raw_composite_score, then caps at 100. Does NOT compare against the entry threshold — that check happens at step 8.4 after all multipliers are applied. Threshold comparison here was removed in v1.3 to eliminate the redundant pre-filter.

conditions.score_formula: raw_composite_score = range_score(T1) + sd_score(T2) + liquidity_score(T3) + tct_5a_score(T4) + tct_5b_score(T5) + advanced_score(T6) + rr_bonus(T7) + session_additive(T8)
conditions.score_cap_rationale: Session additive can push raw score above 100 in peak session (e.g. perfect setup in London/NY overlap: 95 tree pts + 10 session = 105 raw → capped at 100). Cap ensures multipliers in step 8.4 always start from a bounded [0,100] base.
conditions.no_threshold_check: Step 7.0 does NOT check against entry threshold. The sole threshold comparison is at step 8.4 after session_multiplier and rig_confidence_factor are applied.

children.name: TREE_1_RANGE_STRUCTURE
children.decision_logic: Scores the quality and validity of the identified consolidation range.

conditions.full_pass: Valid horizontal range with EQ touched and liquidity stacking → 20 pts
conditions.partial_pass: Range found but quality concerns → 10-15 pts
conditions.soft_fail: Range validation issues → 5 pts (does not reject, just low score)

children.name: TREE_2_SUPPLY_DEMAND
children.decision_logic: Scores the presence and quality of supply/demand zones near the entry area.

conditions.full_pass: FVG present and unmitigated in correct zone → 20 pts
conditions.hard_gate_fail: No FVG at all → HARD FAIL (overrides score, entire trade rejected)

children.name: TREE_3_LIQUIDITY
children.decision_logic: Scores the quality of the liquidity sweep event.

conditions.full_pass: Clean sweep classified and accepted back → 15 pts
conditions.partial_pass: Deep break but accepted back → 8-10 pts

children.name: TREE_4_TCT_5A
children.decision_logic: Scores the TCT 5A schematic pattern recognition quality.

children.name: TREE_5_TCT_5B_REAL_WORLD
children.decision_logic: Scores real-world execution factors — how clean the setup looks on actual charts vs theoretical patterns.

conditions.full_pass: Clean taps, good BOS distance (≥ 0.2%), proper spacing → 15 pts

children.name: TREE_6_ADVANCED_FLIP_ESCALATION
children.decision_logic: Bonus scoring for advanced pattern recognition — schematic flips, escalation setups, and multi-timeframe confluence signals.

children.name: RR_BONUS
children.decision_logic: Awards bonus points for excellent risk-to-reward ratios.

children.name: SESSION_TIMING_ADJUSTMENT
children.decision_logic: Determines the active trading session from the current UTC hour and returns both a session score additive (added directly to composite_score) and a session confidence multiplier (used in step 8.4). NOT a hard gate — but the multiplier in step 8.4 CAN cause a trade to fail threshold after adjustment.
children._annotation: MSCE v1.1 — Step 7.8 now outputs both a score additive (unchanged from v1.0) and a session_multiplier. The multiplier is NOT applied here; it is passed to step 8.4 for adjusted_confidence computation. Score additives in conditions match constants.MSCE_SESSION_MULTIPLIERS[session].score_additive.

conditions._session_windows_note: Windows are non-overlapping UTC ranges. london_ny is evaluated BEFORE london and ny to capture the overlap correctly.

london_ny_overlap.label: London/NY overlap — peak liquidity
london_ny_overlap.rationale: Both European and US institutions active. Highest historical follow-through rate on BOS signals. Maximum confidence.

ny_session.label: NY session — strong confidence
ny_session.rationale: US institutional flow dominant after London close. Clean directional moves, reliable FVG fills.

london_session.label: London session — elevated confidence
london_session.rationale: European open ramps volume. Range breakouts carry more weight than Asia. Slightly lower than NY due to frequent reversals at London close.

asia_session.label: Asia session — reduced confidence
asia_session.rationale: Low liquidity, thin books. Stop-hunt deviations are common but reversal follow-through is weak. Higher false-positive rate on BOS. Multiplier intentionally conservative.

off_hours.label: NY close / off-hours — slightly reduced confidence
off_hours.rationale: Position squaring at NY close creates noise. Setups often reverse at Asia open. Small penalty applied.

msce_multiplier_table._note: Reference table matching constants.MSCE_SESSION_MULTIPLIERS. Source of truth is the constants block.

children.name: TRADE_EXECUTION
children._annotation: v1.3 — Removed the redundant composite_score >= threshold pre-filter that was here in v1.2. Step 8.4 (EXECUTION_CONFIDENCE_AGGREGATION) is now the sole and authoritative threshold gate. This step receives composite_score and all multiplier inputs and routes directly to 8.4 before sizing or placement.
children.decision_logic: Routes to execution sub-steps in order: 8.4 (confidence aggregation + threshold gate) → 8.1 (position sizing) → 8.2 (liquidation check) → 8.3 (order placement). If step 8.4 rejects, the pipeline stops and the scan cycle continues. No raw threshold pre-check here — execution_confidence is the only gate.

conditions.entry_route: All trades reaching step 8.0 have passed all 7 hard gates (step 6.0). Proceed directly to step 8.4 for confidence aggregation.
conditions.sole_threshold_gate: execution_confidence (step 8.4) >= entry_threshold → trade executes. Below threshold → NO TRADE, log confidence_components, continue scanning.
conditions._removed_v1_3: Pre-filter 'composite_score >= threshold' removed. Rationale: with the RIG penalty factor, a raw score above threshold may still fail after multipliers are applied, and conversely a London/NY overlap trade with a lower raw score may still pass. Pre-filtering on raw score would incorrectly reject valid setups.

children.name: POSITION_SIZING
children.decision_logic: Calculates position size based on fixed 1% risk per trade. Position = (balance × risk%) / |entry - stop| × leverage. Ensures margin does not exceed available balance.

conditions.margin_check: margin = position_size / leverage; must be ≤ available_balance
conditions.no_dynamic_scaling: Risk is FIXED at 1% — does not adjust based on confidence, score, or MSCE session multiplier. The MSCE multiplier affects ONLY the execution confidence gate (step 8.4), never position size.

children.name: LIQUIDATION_SAFETY_CHECK
children.decision_logic: Calculates the estimated liquidation price and verifies it is safely OUTSIDE the stop loss. This is non-negotiable per TCT Lecture 9 — liquidation must never be hit before stop loss.

conditions.long_danger: liquidation_price >= stop_price → DANGER: reduce leverage or reject
conditions.short_danger: liquidation_price <= stop_price → DANGER: reduce leverage or reject

children.name: ORDER_PLACEMENT
children.decision_logic: Generates the final order specification. Primary entry is MARKET order (95% of entries — at BOS confirmation). Limit orders used for partial take-profits.
outputs: order_spec: {type, side, size, price, sl, tp}

conditions.limit_alternative: LIMIT at refined LTF price — 5% of trades (better entry but may miss)

children.name: EXECUTION_CONFIDENCE_AGGREGATION
children._annotation: v1.3 — Renamed and expanded from MSCE_EXECUTION_CONFIDENCE_ADJUSTMENT. Now aggregates ALL confidence factors in a defined, sequential formula: composite_score cap → session multiplier (MSCE) → RIG confidence penalty. This is the single authoritative threshold gate. The redundant pre-check that was in step 8.0 has been removed. Position sizing (step 8.1) is never touched.
children.decision_logic: Applies the three-factor execution confidence formula in order. First caps the score (already done at step 7.0, verified here). Then applies the MSCE session multiplier. Then applies the RIG confidence factor based on how the trade cleared the Range Integrity Gate. The product is execution_confidence. This single value is compared against the entry threshold — the ONLY threshold check in the pipeline.
outputs: confidence_components: object (shows each factor and intermediate value)

conditions._formula_chain: Three sequential multipliers applied to produce execution_confidence:
conditions.step_1_cap: capped_score = min(composite_score, 100)  — defense against raw scores > 100 (already applied at step 7.0; verified here)
conditions.step_2_session: session_adjusted = capped_score × session_multiplier  — MSCE: rewards high-volume sessions, penalizes low-liquidity sessions
conditions.step_3_rig: execution_confidence = session_adjusted × rig_confidence_factor  — RIG: penalizes counter-bias trades based on how they cleared the gate

rig_factor_lookup._source: constants.RIG_CONFIDENCE_PENALTIES — canonical values; lookup key = rig_verdict_path prefix (before any ':' character)
rig_factor_lookup._fallback: If rig_verdict_path is unrecognised → use 1.00 and log warning (fail-safe: do not penalise unknown paths)

conditions.threshold_check: execution_confidence >= entry_threshold → PASS → proceed to order placement
conditions.threshold_fail: execution_confidence < entry_threshold → REJECT → log full confidence_components breakdown
conditions.no_position_size_scaling: execution_confidence controls ONLY the entry gate. The 1% fixed risk rule (step 8.1) is invariant — position size and risk % are never adjusted by session or RIG factors.
conditions.log_always: Always emit confidence_components regardless of pass/fail. Required for diagnostic dashboard (step 14.0) and backtesting.
conditions.session_fallback: If active_session cannot be resolved from UTC hour → use session_multiplier=1.00 and log warning
conditions.rig_fallback: If rig_verdict_path is absent (e.g. RIG was not reached due to fast-pass) → use rig_confidence_factor=1.00

confidence_components_schema._note: Structure of the confidence_components object emitted for every evaluation.

children.name: TRADE_MANAGEMENT
children.decision_logic: Once a trade is open, manages it through its lifecycle: partial take-profit at midpoint, trailing stop to breakeven, and final exit at TP or SL.

conditions.runs_every: Checked on each 60s scan cycle while position is open

children.name: PARTIAL_TAKE_PROFIT
children.decision_logic: When price reaches the midpoint between entry and target, close 50% of the position to lock in profit.

children.name: TRAILING_STOP_TO_BREAKEVEN
children.decision_logic: After partial TP is taken, move the stop loss to breakeven (entry price) to eliminate risk on remaining position.

conditions.trigger: After partial TP is closed → move SL to entry_price

children.name: FINAL_EXIT
children.decision_logic: Remaining position exits at full target (TP), secondary target (TP2), or stop loss (SL). Trade is then logged and state is cleared for next scan.

conditions.tp_hit_long: live_price >= target_price → close remaining at profit
conditions.tp_hit_short: live_price <= target_price → close remaining at profit
conditions.sl_hit_long: live_price <= stop_price → close at loss (or breakeven if trailed)
conditions.sl_hit_short: live_price >= stop_price → close at loss (or breakeven if trailed)
conditions.tp2_optional: If tp2_price set → close last portion at tp2 instead of tp1

children.name: STATE_PERSISTENCE_AND_RECOVERY
children.decision_logic: Persists all trade state to disk (JSON) and optionally syncs to GitHub data branch. On restart, loads persisted state, validates it, and resumes operation.

conditions.save_triggers: After every trade entry, partial TP, exit, and balance change
conditions.corruption_detection: On load: if entry_price=0 OR stop_price=0 OR target_price=0 → discard trade (treat as no open position)
conditions.github_sync: If GITHUB_TOKEN_2 env var present → push to data branch automatically

workflow.name: HTF_UPGRADE_MECHANISM
workflow.decision_logic: After finding a schematic on one timeframe, checks if a higher timeframe also shows a valid schematic at the same level. If so, upgrades the analysis to the HTF for more significant structure. Walks up the TF hierarchy: 30m → 1h → 4h → 1d.

conditions.upgrade_criteria: HTF schematic found at overlapping price levels AND same direction

workflow.name: ALTERNATIVE_EVALUATION_MODE_JACK
workflow.decision_logic: Alternative 5-tree evaluation pipeline that uses only the 4H timeframe. Scoring weights: market_structure=20, ranges=20, s/d=20, liquidity=15, tct_5a=25. Threshold = 50. Has its own set of hard gates (MS valid, FVG present, 5A confirmed).

conditions.activation: Selected via evaluator mode config (not default)
conditions.4h_exclusive: Only analyzes 4H timeframe — no LTF cascade

workflow.name: PO3_SCHEMATIC_MODE
workflow.decision_logic: Power of Three (PO3) detection from TCT Lecture 8. Identifies three-phase pattern: Range (accumulation/distribution) → Manipulation (liquidity sweep) → Expansion (breakout in intended direction). Uses RTZ quality scoring and stricter R:R minimum of 2.0.

conditions.phase_1_range: Horizontal consolidation identified (reuses range logic from step 3.0)
conditions.phase_2_manipulation: Aggressive breakout that stays within DL2 (false breakout / liquidity grab)
conditions.phase_3_expansion: Directional move in intended direction after manipulation
conditions.min_rr: R:R >= 2.0 (stricter than standard TCT minimum of 1.5)

workflow.name: NOTIFICATIONS_AND_DIAGNOSTICS
workflow.decision_logic: Sends notifications on key events (trade entry, partial TP, exit) via Telegram. Exposes diagnostic data via FastAPI endpoints and WebSocket for the live dashboard. Maintains last_debug state for UI rendering.

conditions.dashboard_updates: Pushed via WebSocket or polling every scan cycle

data_flow_summary.2_structure_analysis: Candles → 6CR Pivots → Swing H/L → HTF Bias
data_flow_summary.5_bos_confirmation: Post-Tap3 candles → MTF BOS → LTF cascade refinement
data_flow_summary.6_gating: All upstream → 7 hard gates (any fail = reject). Gate 6.7 (RIG): if counter-bias trade + intact HTF range → check CHoCH domino bypass → apply session-adjusted displacement threshold → PASS or HARD FAIL
data_flow_summary.7_scoring: Gate results → trees T1-T6 + rr_bonus(T7) + session_additive(T8) → raw_score(max 110) → capped composite_score(max 100). session_multiplier passed through to step 8.4.
data_flow_summary.8_execution: [8.4 EXECUTION_CONFIDENCE_AGGREGATION: execution_confidence = min(composite_score,100) × session_multiplier × rig_confidence_factor → single threshold gate (60 v2 / 50 v1)] → position sizing (fixed 1%) → liquidation check → order placement
data_flow_summary.8_confidence_formula_note: Three-factor formula: (1) score cap at 100, (2) MSCE session multiplier (0.85-1.25), (3) RIG penalty factor (0.85-1.00 based on how the trade cleared gate 6.7). No other threshold checks exist in the pipeline.
data_flow_summary.9_management: Open position → partial TP → trailing SL → final exit
data_flow_summary.10_persistence: All state → JSON on disk + GitHub sync

critical_failure_modes.description: HTF bias is neutral — v2 treats this as hard fail, preventing any entries during unclear market structure
critical_failure_modes.impact: May miss valid setups during transitional periods

critical_failure_modes.description: No FVG found near Tap3 — hard gate rejection even if all other criteria pass
critical_failure_modes.impact: FVG detection sensitivity directly controls entry frequency

critical_failure_modes.description: BOS staleness exceeds MAX_STALE candles — valid schematic rejected because confirmation came too late
Liquidation price inside stop loss — trade rejected at execution stage despite passing all scoring
critical_failure_modes.impact: Leverage too high for the risk distance — requires manual leverage adjustment

critical_failure_modes.description: Counter-bias entry blocked by RIG when trend shift IS occurring but CHoCH not yet confirmed across all 3 levels (L3→L2→L1 domino not complete)
critical_failure_modes.impact: Early trend shifts may be systematically missed. Partial bypass (2-of-3 levels) halves the displacement threshold as a mitigation, but the full RIG block remains until L3 confirms.
critical_failure_modes.mitigation: Watch for CHoCH level count in diagnostic output. If L1 and L2 are confirmed but L3 is pending, the partial bypass is active. If model-flow conversion (Model 1→2 or schematic flip) occurs, bypass is granted independently of CHoCH.

critical_failure_modes.description: RIG fast-pass for 'range_intact == False' may let counter-bias trades through when range has TECHNICALLY been violated but is actually just re-accumulating
critical_failure_modes.impact: A single DL2 close violation that gets accepted back in ≤3 candles is classified as a sweep (not a violation) — so the range remains intact. But if 4+ candles elapse before return, it's a violation and the range loses protection. Re-accumulation patterns may be misclassified as broken ranges.
critical_failure_modes.mitigation: RIG_DL2_VIOLATION_LOOKBACK_CANDLES (default 3) controls this boundary. Tuning upward (e.g., to 5) would retain range protection longer but risks treating true breaks as sweeps.

critical_failure_modes.description: Session threshold for Asia (0.15) may be too tight, filtering valid counter-trend entries during Asian range breakouts that precede London momentum
critical_failure_modes.impact: Pre-London momentum setups that form during Asia session (00:00–07:00 UTC) and represent genuine structure shifts will be blocked unless displacement reaches 15%. This 15% threshold was chosen conservatively and may need calibration against backtested Asia session data.
critical_failure_modes.mitigation: RIG_DISPLACEMENT_SESSION_THRESHOLDS.asia is a tunable constant. If backtesting shows valid Asia counter-bias setups being blocked, raise to 0.18–0.20.
